title: coris monspeliensis subsp. somaliensis
intro:
Coris monspeliensis subsp. somaliensis, also known as Somali rock sage, is a subspecies of the Coris monspeliensis plant, a member of the Lamiaceae family, native to the Horn of Africa.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oil composition.

The health benefits of Coris monspeliensis subsp. somaliensis include its potential use as an antiseptic, a treatment for wounds, and a remedy for various respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis.

In traditional medicine, this plant is prepared as a tea or infused in oil for various herbal preparations, including topical applications for skin and wound care, and internal use as a treatment for various health conditions.
